All works

Chipolo
One

A CMF redesign exercise for the Chipolo One Bluetooth tracker — exploring how colour, material, and finish choices can reposition a functional object as something genuinely desirable.

Year
2023
Disciplines
CMF Design 3D Modelling Rendering Brand Study
Tools
Rhinoceros 3D Keyshot Adobe Illustrator
Overview

The Brief

Chipolo One is a Bluetooth tracker — small, circular, and immediately recognisable by the embossed wordmark on its face. This project is a CMF exploration: taking an existing form and asking what a new colour and finish direction might do for its personality and market positioning.

The existing range uses saturated primaries. The new direction explores a softer, more considered palette — one that sits comfortably in contemporary interiors rather than screaming for attention on a keyring.

Sage, coral and lemon — the three soft colourways in context

Monochrome scene studies — each colourway staged against a matching environment

Challenge

Working Within the Form

The Chipolo One's geometry is fixed — a 37mm disc with an embossed logo and a keyring hole. Every decision in this project had to work within that constraint. CMF became the entire design space.

The challenge was to define a palette that felt cohesive across six colourways, worked as a monochromatic story (body and logo in the same hue), and still read as distinct and individual units within the range.

Six colourways: blue, yellow, coral, white, sage, asphalt

Process

Developing the Range

The process began with a colour mood study, drawing references from homeware, fashion accessories, and stationery — categories where colour is a primary purchase driver. Saturated primaries were set aside early in favour of colours with more grey in them: warmer, quieter, easier to live with.

Each colourway was modelled and rendered in a monochromatic environment to test the body-environment relationship, then brought together in group compositions to evaluate how the range works as a family.
